title: A fast heat pipe design and analysis methodology
abstract: The invention relates to a method for performance determination of a heat pipe with arbitrary liquid flow area and prescribed geometric dimensions, external and internal structure, heat pipe material and working fluid, heating and cooling surface areas, condenser cooling conditions to obtain operating and performance parameters (temperature distribution within the heat pipe, the heat transferred via phase change and conduction, the axial variation of the radius of curvature of the liquid-vapor interface along the heat pipe, the vapor temperature and pressure of the working fluid) by simulating the flow and energy transfer inside.
claims:
Claims 1. A fast performance determination and design methodology simulating the flow and energy transfer inside the heat pipe, characterized in that the methodology comprises the following steps: i. Calculation of the pressure variation (Pu q ) along the heat pipe and corresponding edge angle variation (0 edge ) to obtain the liquid and vapor phases distribution in the heat pipe wick structure; ii. Determining the length of the effective liquid flow region (L e f ), which extends between the initial point in the evaporator section, where the effective liquid flow occurs, and the point in the condenser section, where the edge angle first reaches 90â iii. Calculation of the amount of heat transferred by conduction (Q cond ) and phase change (Q pc ), using the conduction thermal resistance (7? cond ) and the thermal resistances in the evaporator section (R e ) and condenser section (R c ) during phase change; iv. Obtaining the operating and performance parameters of the heat pipe, such as the temperature distribution along the heat pipe, the variation of the radius of curvature of the liquid-vapor interface along the heat pipe, the vapor temperature, and pressure of the fluid. 2. The performance determination and design methodology according to Claim 1 , characterized in that, performance parameters for designs with varying cross- sectional area/geometry and/or capillary size and/or hierarchical topography and/or porosity and permeability for heat pipes with porous wick structure along the heat pipe are obtained. 3. The performance determination and design methodology according to Claim 1 , characterized in that, the methodology relates to a process step of determining the initial point of dryout and conducting the performance analysis in the presence of dryout region, if any, in the evaporator section. 

The performance determination and design methodology according to Claim 1 , characterized in that, the methodology relates to a process step of determining the formation of a liquid pool due to the accumulation of liquid in the condenser section and performing the analysis in the presence of a liquid pool, if any.
